page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores built on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard operations. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 12133.23 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5344.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth reaching 472.5 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write bandwidths measure 101.11 GB/sec and 47.4 GB/sec respectively. However, single-threaded performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 930.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 6.72 GB/sec.

The combination of high memory capacity and integrated local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance class highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring rapid data access without the overhead of network-attached storage. Database operations perform well, with Passmark database operations scoring 5295.54 and Redis SET operations reaching 9.53 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also stable at 9.91 GB/sec. Conversely, the instance is less suited for heavy computational tasks like LLM inference, where text generation speeds for a 7B model drop to 6.76 tokens per second. Recommended workloads include in-memory databases, caching layers, and medium-scale data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by the 2.5 GHz ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show average multi-core and database performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 9.53 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 9.91 GB/sec. However, single-threaded performance and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and web serving workloads that benefit from local high-speed SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 8 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 474 GB local NVMe SSD storage, ideal for databases and caching layers.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
474 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
9.53 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and web serving
